description: C0010 - UNC3890 infrastructure preparation, watering-hole staging, and tool delivery chain aligned with the legacy Docker corpus
objective: Reproduce the legacy nine-technique C0010 chain with controlled infrastructure staging and lab-safe watering-hole interaction.

techniques.name: Domains
techniques.description: During C0010, UNC3890 actors likely compromised the domain of a legitimate Israeli shipping company.
techniques.procedure_summary: Represent compromise of a legitimate shipping-domain asset through a lab-safe structured manifest.

techniques.name: Upload Malware
techniques.description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors staged malware on their infrastructure for direct download onto a compromised system.
techniques.procedure_summary: Stage a benign payload artifact that stands in for malware prepared on attacker-controlled infrastructure.

techniques.name: Drive-by Target
techniques.description: For C0010, the threat actors compromised the login page of a legitimate Israeli shipping company and likely established a watering hole that collected visitor information.
techniques.procedure_summary: Stage a benign landing page and client-side script to represent the compromised watering-hole target.

techniques.name: Drive-by Compromise
techniques.description: During C0010, UNC3890 actors likely established a watering hole that was hosted on a login page of a legitimate Israeli shipping company that was active until at least November 2021.
techniques.procedure_summary: Fetch the staged watering-hole page and its client-side script over HTTP without delivering an exploit.

techniques.name: Malware
techniques.description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors used unique malware, including SUGARUSH and SUGARDUMP.
techniques.procedure_summary: Capture malware-development intent through a benign capability specification rather than a live payload.

techniques.name: Domains
techniques.description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors established domains that appeared to be legitimate services and entities, such as LinkedIn, Facebook, Office 365, and Pfizer.
techniques.procedure_summary: Represent lookalike-domain acquisition through a controlled infrastructure artifact.

techniques.name: Upload Tool
techniques.description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors staged tools on their infrastructure to download directly onto a compromised system.
techniques.procedure_summary: Stage a benign tool artifact for later transfer into the compromised environment.

techniques.name: Tool
techniques.description: For C0010, UNC3890 actors obtained multiple publicly-available tools, including METASPLOIT, UNICORN, and NorthStar C2.
techniques.procedure_summary: Build a benign toolkit artifact that stands in for publicly obtained offensive tooling.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: During C0010, UNC3890 actors downloaded tools and malware onto a compromised host.
techniques.procedure_summary: Transfer a benign payload over HTTP within the lab substrate to preserve ingress-tool behavior.
